key_insight: A physics-informed CNN-LSTM can forecast long-term photoluminescence degradation of metal halide perovskites from early time-series data, even for unseen samples.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, lower-cost stability screening of candidate semiconductors, helping materials engineers prioritize robust perovskites earlier in the discovery pipeline.
method: Collected a diverse dataset of 167 perovskite samples under heat and illumination stress.
Featurized >86k PL spectra with a physics-informed representation.
Trained a hybrid CNN-LSTM to predict future PL intensity and generalize across material groups.
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on unseen samples and reportedly outperformed baseline benchmarks while maintaining explainability through physics-based features.
watch_item: Forecast accuracy may depend on how well the training data cover the full range of compositions, crystallinities, and degradation behaviors encountered in deployment.
paper_title: Physics-informed time-series forecasting of perovskite photoluminescence stability
